user_query:
I’m setting up a small bilingual website for our Slovenian community in Argentina and I want a reproducible pre-deploy step that both updates our config and pulls a couple of authoritative web pages we reference.

Please do the following using only local scripts and standard tools, and keep all outputs under the workspace/ folder:

1) Inspect and update input/config.yaml:
   - Set site.default_locale to "sl".
   - Ensure site.enabled_locales contains both "sl" and "es-AR" (no duplicates). If you choose to sort, use alphabetical order.
   - Set content.license.license_sections_path to "web/cc_by_sections.json" (path relative to the workspace root, no leading slash).

2) Implement a pre-deploy script at workspace/scripts/predeploy.sh (shell entrypoint; it can call Python/Node/etc.) that performs BOTH web retrievals and extractions below. Re-running the script should deterministically overwrite/update the same output files.

A) License page extraction:
   - From the official Creative Commons website (creativecommons.org), download the English legalcode page for "Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)" and save the raw HTML to workspace/web/raw/cc-by-4.0-legalcode.html.
   - Using the CSS selectors defined in input/sample_html_patterns.yaml (cc_by_legalcode.heading_selectors and cc_by_legalcode.paragraph_selector), parse the saved HTML and build a structured JSON at workspace/web/cc_by_sections.json where each element has:
     { "section_id": slug from the heading text (lowercase, words joined by dashes, strip non-alphanumerics), "heading": heading text, "text": concatenated paragraph text belonging to that heading, "length_chars": character length of text }.
     Treat each h2/h3 as a new section; include paragraphs until the next section heading.
   - Create a ranked CSV at workspace/web/cc_by_sections_ranked.csv with columns: rank,section_id,heading,length_chars. Rank sections by length_chars descending; ties break by section_id ascending.

B) Link domain counting from Slovenia’s government site:
   - From the main site of the Government of Slovenia (gov.si), download the homepage HTML and save it to workspace/web/raw/gov-si-homepage.html.
   - Using the selector in input/sample_html_patterns.yaml (gov_homepage.link_selector), extract absolute links. Keep only links whose host ends with any suffix listed in input/config.yaml under web.link_domains_whitelist (currently [".si", ".gov.ar"]). Normalize hosts to lowercase.
   - Produce workspace/web/domain_counts.csv with columns: domain,count listing each host and the number of occurrences found on the page, sorted by count descending, then domain ascending as a tiebreaker.

Requirements and notes:
- Do not hardcode URLs in the code output; locate the target pages by their official source, document title, and domain. Save the raw HTML artifacts exactly at the specified paths.
- Make the predeploy.sh script idempotent and suitable to run before a static site build.
- Keep the existing YAML structure in input/config.yaml; only change the values requested.
- All filesystem paths in this request are relative to the workspace root.
- Deliverables to produce/update:
  - input/config.yaml (updated values as above)
  - workspace/scripts/predeploy.sh (executable)
  - workspace/web/raw/cc-by-4.0-legalcode.html
  - workspace/web/cc_by_sections.json
  - workspace/web/cc_by_sections_ranked.csv
  - workspace/web/raw/gov-si-homepage.html
  - workspace/web/domain_counts.csv All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
